summary: Iteration 108: Testing 6-frame grid feature for storyboard panels. Backend: 13/13 tests passed (100%). Frontend code review confirms correct implementation of 3-column grid rendering with frame labels on hover.
critical_code_review_comments: StoryboardEditor.jsx line 406-418: 6-frame grid renders when panel.frames?.length > 1 using grid-cols-3 layout
StoryboardEditor.jsx line 409-416: Each frame renders with image_url, label on hover (opacity-0 to opacity-100 transition)
StoryboardEditor.jsx line 419-424: Falls back to single image when panel has no frames (existing panels)
StoryboardEditor.jsx line 428: Shows 'Gerando 6 frames...' text during regeneration
Backend storyboard.py line 132-165: _split_grid_into_frames() correctly splits 2x3 grid using Pillow
Backend storyboard.py line 56-85: Prompt asks Gemini for 2x3 grid with 6 specific panel types
Backend storyboard.py line 246-258: Frame labels defined as ['Plano Geral', 'Close-up', 'Ação', 'Reação', 'Ângulo Dramático', 'Transição']
Backend studio.py line 1076-1103: regenerate-panel endpoint also produces 6 frames via _split_grid_into_frames()

success_rate.frontend: Code review passed - all features correctly implemented

seed_data_creation: Used existing project fce897cf6ba3 - Panel 1 has 6 frames (regenerated with new feature), Panels 2-20 have 0 frames (single image fallback)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 108 tested the 6-frame grid feature: 1) Backend API returns panels with 'frames' array containing 6 items for panel 1, 2) Each frame has frame_number, image_url, label fields, 3) _split_grid_into_frames() correctly splits 2x3 grid, 4) Frontend renders 3-column grid when frames exist, falls back to single image otherwise. Browser automation had difficulty navigating to storyboard page due to complex multi-step navigation (Marketing > Criar com AI Studio > Project > Storyboard step), but code review confirms all UI elements are correctly implemented.

backend_api.GET /api/studio/projects/{id}/storyboard: PASS - Returns panels with frames array

backend_functions._split_grid_into_frames with mock image: PASS - Correctly splits 600x900 into 6 frames of 300x300

frontend_code_review.Single image fallback: PASS - When panel has no frames
